page: Microsoft Azure E32as_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Easv7 server family. It runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and is powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with an x86_64 architecture. The configuration includes 16 physical cores and 32 virtual CPUs (vCPUs) with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The CPU features 48 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache, alongside advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and SHA_NI. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU testing and single-threaded gzip compression both ranking in the top 10%. Multi-core capabilities are strong, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth benchmarks for both cached small blocks and uncached large blocks fall within the average range, while the PassMark memory score ranks in the top 25%. For specialized workloads, the server demonstrates strong prompt processing and top-tier text generation speeds for small (135M) language models, though performance drops to average for medium (7B) models and poor (bottom 10%) for large (70B) model text generation.

This server does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, though it is Premium Storage capable, requiring external network-attached storage for persistent data. The absence of a GPU limits its utility for heavy deep learning training, but its high memory density and strong single-core and multi-core CPU performance offer qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads. It fits memory-intensive applications, database operations, caching layers like Redis, and small-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E32as_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Easv7 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, it features 16 physical cores, 32 vCPUs, and 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core capabilities, alongside average memory bandwidth. While it lacks local storage and a GPU, it supports Premium Storage. This configuration is efficient for memory-intensive workloads, including database operations, caching layers, and small-scale language model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32as_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V45 processor and 256.0 GB of RAM, delivering top-tier single-core CPU performance for memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high-frequency AMD EPYC processor and 256 GB of RAM for memory-intensive workloads.

256.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core benchmark results
No local storage included, but compatible with Premium Storage options
Fits memory-intensive databases, caching, and small-scale LLM inference
